hero.label: Made for Double
hero.headline: I want to be the SDR who books the deals that fund your next 100 bookkeepers.
hero.subheadline: Double is building the operating system for the 1.5M bookkeepers in the US — the software Pilot wrote internally, available to everyone. That's a massive TAM and a distribution problem that needs great pipeline. I want to be the person building that pipeline from the ground up.

whyCompany.title: You're attacking a $400B back-office market that nobody talks about
whyCompany.body: Pilot got valued at $1.2B doing bookkeeping for startups. Double is selling the picks and shovels to the 1.5M independent bookkeepers who do this work for everyone else. That's the smarter play — and it's a story I can tell convincingly in a cold call.

whyCompany.title: 80 people and growing — this is the moment to join
whyCompany.body: YC S21 means you've had years to figure out the product and early customers. 80 people means you're past survival but still early enough that an SDR can make a real mark on pipeline. That's the stage I want to be in.

whyCompany.title: I've done exactly this — built B2B pipeline from zero at a startup
whyCompany.body: At Copygen.ai, I was the first BDR — no playbook, no warm leads, just a product and a target list. I used HubSpot, LinkedIn sequences, and cold outreach to help acquire 15-20 paying B2B clients. The mechanics translate directly to what you need here.

quote: I've watched founders try to figure out bookkeeping in 15 different spreadsheets. Double solves that. I want to be the one calling the bookkeepers who could build their whole practice around your software.
